title: Classifier type
description: Configure type = CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION for classification routes and classifier-specific output restrictions through a HOSTED_WORKER companion.
openingSummary: CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION is the WorkstationType for label-based classificationRoutes with restricted success outputs. Set type to CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION when a step must route by classification labels instead of outputs, onContinue, or onRejection, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling. Keep guards on LOGICAL_MOVE instead.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Classifier is the Factory WorkstationType for classification routes and classifier-specific output restrictions in you-agent-factory. This page covers the type = CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION discriminator, how CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION pairs with a HOSTED_WORKER companion and the exclusive classificationRoutes field, the validated type overlay embed, a minimal valid configuration, an incompatible misuse that borrows ordinary outputs routing, operational cautions, and compatible WorkstationKind behaviors. Use it when a workstation must route by classification labels and must not declare outputs, onContinue, or onRejection.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: The discriminator field is type with value CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION. CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION selects the runtime that routes work through classificationRoutes and excludes ordinary outputs, onContinue, and onRejection success paths. Type is independent of behavior: CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION pairs with every published WorkstationKind, including STANDARD and REPEATER. CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION is not a scheduling behavior and is not LOGICAL_MOVE — keep guards on the logical-move type. Both share HOSTED_WORKER companions, but their selected field sets stay separate.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION and bind a HOSTED_WORKER companion. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once classification, REPEATER for change-triggered loops, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus classificationRoutes so each label maps to destination workstations, and keep onFailure for failure routing. Do not set outputs, onContinue, onRejection, guards, openCodeAgent, promptFile, outcomeFormat, operation, operationBindings, or cron fields that belong to other WorkstationType or WorkstationKind families. Choose LOGICAL_MOVE for guarded non-worker routing, AGENT_RUN for agent loops, or INFERENCE_RUN for bounded inference harness dispatch.

variantFields.title: Variant Fields
variantFields.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION overlay applicability. CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION selects the exclusive classificationRoutes field and excludes outputs, onContinue, and onRejection. Other excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION with behavior STANDARD and classificationRoutes. The misuse example borrows outputs from ordinary WorkstationTypes — that field is excluded from CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION.

operationalCautions.title: Operational Cautions
operationalCautions.body: CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION steps fail closed when the bound worker is missing or is not a HOSTED_WORKER, when classificationRoutes are missing or leave a label unmapped, when concurrency or resource limits refuse a run, when onFailure leaves a failure outcome unmapped, or when authors place ordinary outputs, logical-move guards, agent-run, model, or cron exclusives on this type. Do not use outputs, onContinue, or onRejection under CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION — those fields belong on non-classifier types. Keep scheduling on behavior and runtime on type.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the direct /docs/workstations/classifier surface for CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION runtime. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not the LOGICAL_MOVE type guide, and not the STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER behavior contracts. Compatible WorkstationKind companions name the scheduling pairing for this type; they do not redefine CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION fields. Worker backends stay on the workers family.

links.companionIntro: CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION requires a HOSTED_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the hosted worker for classification routing, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis. LOGICAL_MOVE also uses HOSTED_WORKER but selects guards instead of classificationRoutes.
links.misuseNote: outputs, onContinue, and onRejection are excluded from CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION. Keep label routing on classificationRoutes and failure routing on onFailure only.
links.failureWorkerMissingCause: No worker bound, or bound worker is not a HOSTED_WORKER — author and bind a hosted backend
links.failureWorkerMismatchCause: Bound worker is AGENT_WORKER, MODEL_WORKER, or another specialized run type — switch to HOSTED_WORKER or change workstation type
links.failureClassificationRoutesMissingCause: classificationRoutes missing or incomplete — author label maps for every classification outcome
links.failureConcurrencyCause: Workstation or resource limits blocked classification routing — raise limits or reduce parallel work
links.failureRoutingCause: Failure outcome has no authored route — wire onFailure for classifier failure paths
links.failureOutputsMisuseCause: outputs, onContinue, or onRejection declared under CLASSIFIER_WORKSTATION — move ordinary success routing to a non-classifier type or use classificationRoutes
